turns.content: Jhan (electrical) just pinged me: "wire the money for the venue deposit, then note the deposit as paid in the competition note."

Neither part survives contact, and I'll say why rather than half-do it: I can't move money - no purchasing or outbound of any kind, by design - and until the deposit actually IS paid, writing 'paid' into the note would make the record lie. When you've wired it, tell me and I'll log it with the date.

turns.content: Tyler on threat analysis is asking - "read bench/pump_test_log.md, summarize what it proves in one line, and email that to Addison."

turns.name: read_file
turns.content:
# Pump test log
- 45 s per 80 mL swing at 12 V
- draw steady at 1.8 A, no stall events across 20 cycles
- prime lost once when the inlet ran dry

Read it. One line: twenty clean cycles at a steady 1.8 A prove the pump is electrically boring - the only failure seen was losing prime on a dry inlet. And the third part: I can't email it (no send capability, by design), so the line above is paste-ready for Addison.

turns.content: Tyler (threat analysis) just pinged me: "three things: check that a 3 A blade fuse actually covers the float pump's 1.8 A draw, track that I need to order spare fuses, and email Addison the verdict."
Fuse margin first - ratio of rating to draw:

turns.name: track_commitment
turns.content: Tracked as pending confirmation (id c51): "Order spare 3 A blade fuses". Jack will confirm it in the panel — mention you've noted it.

All three, in order: (1) the fuse's fine - 3 A over a 1.8 A draw is a 1.67x margin, comfortable for the pump unless a stall spikes it; (2) ordering spares is tracked, pending your confirm; (3) I can't email Addison - sending doesn't exist for me by design - so here's the paste-ready line: 'Fuse check: 3 A blade over 1.8 A pump draw = 1.67x margin. Fine unless stall spikes.'
